fixture.prompt: People abandon restaurant waitlists. Help me decide whether there is a real product here.
fixture.rationale: Forcing questions and demand-first product pressure.

baseline_invocation.input: People abandon restaurant waitlists. Help me decide whether there is a real product here.

candidate_invocation.input: People abandon restaurant waitlists. Help me decide whether there is a real product here.

mechanical_port.allowed_difference: Canonical GStack 2 carve: exclude the retired shared onboarding wrapper and host hook advisory; resolve retired invocations to six public routes; relocate host/runtime paths; lazy-load pinned carved sections from package-local references.

differences.explanation:
### User-language rule

Write questions, progress updates, reports, and artifacts in the language used by the user. Source material, code identifiers, commands, and quotations may remain in their original language when translating them would reduce accuracy.

### Signal-gated learning

Persist a learning only when the interaction contains a useful, reusable signal such as an explicit preference, correction, accepted recommendation, or rejected direction. Track helpful and harmful outcomes separately. Do not manufacture a learning merely because a workflow completed.

questions.evidence: The candidate exactly matches the deterministic canonical specialist render of the pinned workflow.
